action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   12345678
  +--------+
 1|..#....#|
 2|#.1x...q|
 3|......y.|
 4|.##...#.|
 5|#..S..#.|
 6|........|
 7|#A##p...|
 8|.....2..|
  +--------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (4,5) at t=0, FACING N.
- You always have a facing direction (N, S, E or W). Each tick you make exactly one move:
  F    = step one cell forward, in the direction you are facing
  L    = turn 90 degrees left, in place (you do not move, but the tick is consumed)
  R    = turn 90 degrees right, in place (you do not move, but the tick is consumed)
  WAIT = do nothing for one tick
  There are no absolute moves: to go "north" you must first be facing N, then move F.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving F into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are (facing unchanged) and
     the tick is wasted. L, R and WAIT never bump.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. This applies even on
     ticks where you only turned or waited. Passing a guard mid-move is safe; only
     ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (4,5) facing N again, the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the
  cancelled moves' ticks are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (2,7): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (5,7); t mod 6=1: (5,6); t mod 6=2: (5,5); t mod 6=3: (6,5); t mod 6=4: (5,5); t mod 6=5: (5,6). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (8,2); t mod 4=1: (7,2); t mod 4=2: (6,2); t mod 4=3: (7,2). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (3,2) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (4,2) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (6,8) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (7,3) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 48 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 6 moves at a time; you have at most 8 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you are at
(2,2) facing N at t=0 and want to reach (3,2), which is to the east. Moving F now would
take you to (2,1) instead. The correct sequence is R (t=1, now facing E, still at
(2,2)), then F (t=2, now at (3,2)). If (3,2) were a gate that is closed at t=2, that F
would BUMP and you would have to WAIT for a tick when it is open.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y) FACING <N|S|E|W>
PLAN: <exactly 6 moves from {F, L, R, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AND the direction you
predict you will be facing AFTER your plan has executed. The referee records your
prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I need to deliver Parcel 1 (pick at 3,2, drop at 4,2) and Parcel 2 (pick at 6,8, drop at 7,3) within 48 ticks (8 plans of 6 moves). I start at (4,5) facing N.
First, I need to get to (3,2).
Current pos: (4,5). Target: (3,2).
Path options: Go West to column 3, then North to row 2.
Map check: (4,5) is open. (3,5) is open. (3,4) is a wall (#). I cannot go North directly from (3,5) to (3,4).
So I must go West from (4,5) to (3,5), then turn South? No, target is row 2 (North).
Let's re-evaluate the map.
Row 4: .##...#.| -> Cells (1,4) to (8,4).
(1,4) .
(2,4) #
(3,4) #
(4,4) .
(5,4) .
(6,4) .
(7,4) #
(8,4) .
I am at (4,5). I want to go to (3,5).
(3,5) is '.' (open).
So I can move West: L (face W), F (to 3,5).
At (3,5), I need to go North to (3,4)? No, (3,4) is a wall.
I need to go around the wall at (3,4).
Wait, target is (3,2).
Path: (4,5) -> (3,5) -> ... -> (3,2).
Since (3,4) is a wall, I cannot go North along column 3.
I must go South from (3,5) to (3,6) to (3,7) to (3,8), then East to column 4, then North to row 2?
Let's check the gates.
Gate A is at (2,7).
Guard P cycle: (5,7)->(5,6)->(5,5)->(6,5)->(5,5)->(5,6).
Guard Q cycle: (8,2)->(7,2)->(6,2)->(7,2).

Let's try a different route.
Start (4,5).
Go South to (4,4) (open). Face S, F.
At (4,4). Go South to (4,3) (open). Face S, F.
At (4,3). Go South to (4,2) (open). Face S, F.
At (4,2). This is the delivery spot for Parcel 1!
Wait, Parcel 1 pickup is (3,2). Delivery is (4,2).
If I go South from (4,2) to (4,1)...
Let's check the map for (4,2).
Row 2: #.1x...q|
(1,2) #
(2,2) .
(3,2) 1 (Pickup)
(4,2) x (Delivery)
(5,2) .
(6,2) .
(7,2) . (Guard Q is at 7,2 when t%4==0? Wait. t=0 for Q is 8,2. t=1 is 7,2. t=2 is 6,2. t=3 is 7,2. t=4 is 8,2. t=5 is 7,2. t=6 is 6,2.)
So at t=0, Q is at (8,2).
At t=6, Q is at (6,2).
If I go (4,5)->(4,4)->(4,3)->(4,2), that takes 3 steps (S, S, S).
Time: t=0 start.
t=1: S -> (4,4).
t=2: S -> (4,3).
t=3: S -> (4,2). Pickup Parcel 1.
At (4,2), I need to deliver Parcel 1. But delivery is at (4,2). So I pick it up AND deliver it instantly?
"if you are on the pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver
action.label: response

action.content:
Your last response could not be processed: missing or malformed POSITION line.
Answer again, using exactly this format (and nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning>
POSITION: (x,y) FACING <N|S|E|W>
PLAN: <exactly 6 moves from {F, L, R, WAIT}, separated by spaces>
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I start at (4,5) facing N. I need to pick up Parcel 1 at (3,2) and deliver it to (4,2). Looking at the map, (3,4) and (2,4) are walls (#), blocking a direct North path from (4,5) to (3,2). However, (4,3) and (4,2) are open. I can move South from (4,5) to (4,4), then South to (4,3), then South to (4,2). This path avoids walls.
Regarding guards:
Guard P cycle: (5,7)->(5,6)->(5,5)->(6,5)->(5,5)->(5,6). At t=1, P is at (5,6). My path is (4,5)->(4,4). I am safe.
Guard Q cycle: (8,2)->(7,2)->(6,2)->(7,2). At t=1, Q is at (7,2). My path is (4,5)->(4,4). I am safe.
At t=3, I arrive at (4,2). This is the delivery spot for Parcel 1. So I pick it up.
At t=3, I am at (4,2). Guard P is at (6,5) (t=3 mod 6 = 3 -> pos (6,5)). Guard Q is at (6,2) (t=3 mod 4 = 3 -> pos (7,2)). I am safe from guards.
To complete the plan, I will move South 3 times to reach (4,2) and pick up/deliver Parcel 1. Then I need to get to Parcel 2 at (6,8) and deliver to (7,3).
From (4,2) (with Parcel 1), I need to go to (6,8).
Path: (4,2) -> (5,2) -> (6,2) -> (6,3) -> (6,4) -> (6,5) [Guard P block at t=3 for P, but P moves to (5,6) at t=4, (5,5) at t=5, (6,5) at t=6].
Wait, if I go (4,2)->(5,2) (F). At t=4, P is at (5,6). Safe.
Then (5,2)->(6,2) (F). At t=5, P is at (5,5). Safe (I am at 6,2).
Then (6,2)->(6,3) (F). At t=6, P is at (5,5). Safe.
Then (6,3)->(6,4) (F). At t=7, P is at (6,5). Safe.
Then (6,4)->(6,5) (F). At t=8, P is at (5,6). Safe.
Then (6,5)->(6,6) (F). At t=9, P is at (5,5). Safe.
Then (6,6)->(6,7) (F). At t=10, P is at (5,6). Safe.
Then (6,7)->(6,8) (F). At t=11, P is at (6,5). Safe.
At (6,8), I pick up Parcel 2.
So a sequence of South, South, South, East, East, East... seems viable.
Let's construct a 6-move plan:
1. F (South) to (4,4). Safe.
2. F (South) to (4,3). Safe.
3. F (South) to (4,2). Arrive Pickup 1. Safe.
4. F (East) to (5,2). Safe.
5. F (East) to (6,2). Safe.
6. F (East) to (7,2). Arrive Guard Q?
Guard Q at t=3 is (7,2).
So at t=3 (end of move 3), I am at (4,2). Q is at (6,2). Safe.
At t=4 (end of move 4), I am at (5,2). Q is at (7,2). Safe.
At t=5 (end of move 5), I am at (6,2). Q is at (6,2). **Caught!**
This plan fails at move 5
action.label: response
